pages.raw_text:
The council last night confirmed
the appointment of Steve Johnston
to the position Of plumbing inspec.
tor to succeed Ethal Allen, jr.,
whom the board of health ousted.


The corporation counsel was in
structed to draft an ordinance pro-
viding increase from $3.50 to $2.75
a day in the pay of chaihmen in the
engineers office. Ss e5


Salary boosts were given to the
building inspector. who Is praised
from $110 to 1925 a month, and to
the free employment agent from $40
to $75 a month.


The following ordinances were
passed: ~.


To pay street car fare of en-
ployes to the city engineers Office;
granting the Northern Pacific Rail-
road Co. the right to build spur
track between Front av. and Main
av., Colfax ST. to Market ST.; paving
alley between Stevens st. to Wash
ington ST.; prohibiting the establish
ment Of livery stables or the main
tenance of more than head OF
horses in a block where two-thirds
of the buildings are residences, un-
less a majority OF the property own
ers consent in writing to the es-
tablishment of such stable.
